rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim is under pressure from the prior-work paragraph: Formaggia and Perotto (2003) already did anisotropic DWR estimation for advection-diffusion, making the 'first algorithm' framing potentially inaccurate or at minimum under-argued. Sharpening the thesis depends on resolving what GoalAniso actually adds.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty delta vs. Formaggia and Perotto is the critical scientific fault line. Their work is described in terms nearly identical to GoalAniso's own method description; the differentiating claim (dual-solution-aligned metric tensors vs. element-wise estimators, or steady-state-specific coupling) is never made explicit. This is a load-bearing gap under either interpretation of 'stronger.'
rationale.R1.6: One natural reading of 'make this stronger' is rhetorical and prose-level: tighter sentences, more forceful framing of results, crisper transition from gap to solution. The final paragraph in particular is weak ('practical option' undersells the quantified gains).
